text.text: One of San Jose‘s newest hottest restaurants. This beautifully design architectural building is located in the heart of downtown San Jose across from accessible parking garage. A great place to celebrate special occasions, impress family or guest visiting SJ or date night. The food was spectacular and well presented, drinks were on point and refreshing. It’s not a cheap place but, it gives those who’re doing well in life a fine dining experience. RESERVATIONS ONLY MADE THROUGH OPEN TABLE ONLINE. THEY DO NOT PICK UP PHONE CALLS!

text.text:
I had a wonderful experience here for a Valentine’s Day plated dinner. At $150 per person for the all-inclusive meal, I felt it was truly worth the price for the quality and service provided in the Bay Area. I also opted for the $75 wine pairing, which I found to be very much worth the additional cost.

The team was incredibly helpful before I even arrived. I reached out regarding Halal and vegetarian needs for my partner, and they were very responsive. While they couldn't offer Halal-certified meat, they provided an early preview of the menu so we could see the extensive vegetarian and pescatarian options available. This level of communication made us feel very welcome and accommodated. They even brought out a signed card for us as we were celebrating our anniversary as well.

The restaurant itself is a beautiful, intimate space. It has low lighting and a "garden" feel inside with trees and greenery that make it feel very special. I really appreciated the layout; there is a clear separation between the bar area and the actual dining tables, so it doesn’t feel like everything is just thrown together in one room. Even the bathrooms were really nice and well-maintained.

The cuisine is a California-Mediterranean fusion. You can really taste those influences in the flavors and the way the meals are prepared. For our entrees, we chose the lamb chops and the branzino, which were easily the highlights of the menu for us.

Between the thoughtful service, the atmosphere, and the excellent wine pairings, Eos & Nyx is a gem. Whether it's for a special occasion like Valentine's Day or just a nice dinner out, I highly recommend it.

text.text:
We came here to celebrate my friend’s birthday. The food was great, and we were lucky enough to try one of their newer menu items.

I can’t remember the exact name, but we especially enjoyed the Hawaiian ribs!

originalText.text:

I can’t remember the exact name, but we especially enjoyed the Hawaiian ribs!

text.text:
Incredible service.  Staff were so friendly and attentive.  Constantly refilling our waters and asking if we want another round of cocktails when we're almost done.

Food was great.  Seasonal.  I spoke with our waitress and she said they draw inspiration from their owners. V they have different ethnic owners and each bring something unique to the menu.  It's not a large menu so they do everything quite well.

We were there for Sunday brunch and there was a live DJ playing some 90s-2000s jams.  Overall great place.

text.text:
Ambiance: lively, fun, great atmosphere

Service:  Reservation was at 1900, seated at 1902.  I informed our server, Cris, of my food allergies- chilis of any sort, oranges, grapefruit, tomatoes.  We ordered drinks at 1915.  Cris, seemed knowledgeable with his drinks and drink pairings.  We then ordered our appetizers and entrees at 1920.  We ordered both since Cris said it was a busy night so it would work out better if we do so.  Waited, waited, waited…. Still no food or drinks at 2000.  Cris told us twice that are food would be coming up during that time.  At about 2005 we get our drinks. There were four of us ladies, two celebrating birthdays.  There was only one server on the mezzanine, a one bartender at the bar. At around 2020, we received our appetizers.  We were surprised because at the point of an hour, and all we get is our appetizers?? While we were waiting the chef sends over a mini ice cream looking appetizer, and after our appetizers our server gave us some pb&j shots.  Cris explained that he was sorry for the wait but it was because of all the allergies.  Soon after our appetizers, we got our entrees.  Yay.  The pastas came out first.  Then the steak.  Initially we wanted the lamb but it could not be adjusted to my allergies so the steak was recommended.  They would alter the sauce and the vegetables for my allergies.  I took a bite of the steak and it tasted good.  But on the second bite I noticed heat.  I looked closer at the dish, and there were red dots in the sauce.  I asked Cris if this was chili flakes?  He took the dish and went to the chef.  Shortly after, I was in immense stomach pain (I have chronic gastritis so certain foods inflame and burn my stomach lining).  My stomach started to swell, and I was lying my head on the table.  I gave Cris a moment, but due to the immense pain my friends suggested I go.  One of them walked me to my car.  After I left, they remade the dish and comped the dish.  Unfortunately, I wasn’t able to try it. This was 11/19, I reached out to Eos and Nyx via phone, website and chat on 11/21 with no response as of the date of this review.
I ended up calling out sick and I’m still in pain.  I also am unable to tolerate eating foods that are not bland and basic.

Food: food was decent.  I had their version of a lychee martini but it did not taste like lychee at all.  The pb&j shot was delicious.  The tagliatelle was great! Gnocchi was not bad, but I am not a fan of gnocchi in general.  Bruschetta was great.  Beet appetizer left much to be desired.

Overall:  how can you tell me that the reason our food took so long was because of allergies, but still poison me with chili??  I wouldn’t go back because the turnaround time for food was slow.
